common.name: Name
common.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete

clusters.title: Clusters
clusters.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete cluster "{name}"
clusters.disconnectConfirm: Are you sure you want to disconnect from cluster "{name}"
clusters.confirmDeleteGroup: Are you sure you want to delete group "{name}"? After deletion, all clusters in this group will become ungrouped.
clusters.validationNameInvalid: Cluster name can contain letters, numbers, Chinese characters, hyphens, and underscores
clusters.validationBrokersInvalid: Cluster address cannot contain spaces, quotes, or commas

topics.title: Topics
topics.topicNameValidation: Only letters, numbers, dots, underscores, and hyphens are allowed
topics.confirmDeleteInput: Type the topic name to confirm
topics.validationTopicNameInvalidChars: Topic name cannot contain spaces, quotes, or commas
topics.validationTopicNameFormat: Topic name can only contain letters, numbers, dots, underscores, and hyphens
topics.validationRetentionMs: retention.ms must be a positive number
topics.validationRetentionBytes: retention.bytes must be a number (use -1 for unlimited)
topics.validationSegmentBytes: segment.bytes must be a positive number

messages.title: Messages
messages.exportTip: Query count is per partition, filtering is applied after fetching
messages.confirmDeleteTopic: Are you sure you want to delete topic "{topic}"? This action cannot be undone

consumerGroups.title: Consumer Groups
consumerGroups.emptyHelp: Click the refresh button above to sync consumer groups from the Kafka cluster
consumerGroups.selectFromNav: Please select a consumer group from the left navigation to view details

topicConsumerGroups.title: Topic Consumer Groups
topicConsumerGroups.description: View all Consumer Groups consuming this Topic
topicConsumerGroups.noData: No Consumer Groups consuming this Topic
topicConsumerGroups.dataNotice: Consumer Groups shown on this page are from database history, offset data is fetched from Kafka in real-time.

settings.title: Settings
settings.flatModeDesc: Display all topics in a flat list
settings.systemTrayDesc: When enabled, close button minimizes to tray; when disabled, close button exits the app
settings.autoLaunchDesc: Automatically start Kafka Manager on boot
settings.author: Author
settings.jsonHighlightDesc: Configure JSON highlight style for message details and send message modal
settings.confirmDeleteTemplate: Are you sure you want to delete this custom template?
settings.importExportDesc: Export or import clusters, favorites and history
settings.operationInProgress: Another import/export operation is already in progress, please wait and try again
settings.importStarted: Import started in background, will complete automatically
settings.feedbackDesc: Share your valuable feedback with us
settings.feedbackPlaceholder: Enter your feedback or suggestions (max 2000 characters)
settings.feedbackConnectionFailed: Cannot connect to server, feedback is temporarily unavailable

layout.confirmDeleteCluster: Are you sure you want to remove cluster "{cluster}"?
layout.confirmDeleteTopic: Are you sure you want to delete topic "{topic}"?
layout.shareCopied: Installer copied to your downloads folder, please open file manager to view

update.networkError: Network request failed, please check your connection and try again
update.browserNotSupported: Check for updates is not supported in browser environment
update.minimizeHint: Download will continue in background after closing this dialog
update.rateLimitExceeded: Access denied, please try again later
update.downloadFailed: Download failed, please check your network connection and try again

mainLayout.footerText: Kafka Manager v0.1.0 - Built with Vue 3 + Tailwind CSS + DaisyUI

navigator.viewClusterTopics: View all topics in this cluster

favorites.title: Topic Favorites
favorites.description: Manage your favorite Topics with group support
favorites.emptyHint: Click the button above to create a group
favorites.confirmDeleteGroup: Are you sure you want to delete this group? Favorites in this group will also be deleted.
favorites.confirmDeleteFavorite: Are you sure you want to delete this favorite?
favorites.createGroupHint: Please create a group in favorite management first

history.title: Browsing History
history.description: Automatically record Topics you have viewed
history.emptyHint: Topics will be automatically recorded here when viewed
history.confirmClear: Are you sure you want to clear all history records?

sentMessageHistory.title: Sent Messages
sentMessageHistory.description: Automatically record messages you have sent
sentMessageHistory.emptyHint: Messages will be automatically recorded here when sent
sentMessageHistory.confirmClear: Are you sure you want to clear all sent message history?

schemaRegistry.title: Schema Registry
schemaRegistry.description: Manage Schema Registry configuration and schemas
schemaRegistry.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this subject and all its versions?
schemaRegistry.selectClusterDesc: Choose a Kafka cluster to manage Schema Registry

globalSidebar.title: Sidebar Navigation
globalSidebar.desc: The left sidebar allows you to quickly switch between clusters, topics, favorites, and consumer groups. Click any item to view details.

globalSearch.title: Topic Search
globalSearch.desc: Search for topic names across all clusters in the top search bar.

globalShare.title: Share Installer
globalShare.desc: Click to copy the installer to your downloads folder, or navigate to the GitHub releases page.

globalLang.title: Switch Language
globalLang.desc: Toggle between Chinese and English interface.

globalTheme.title: Toggle Theme
globalTheme.desc: Switch between light and dark themes.

globalSettings.title: Settings
globalSettings.desc: Open the settings page to manage language, theme, sidebar mode, and other global configurations.

clustersAdd.title: Add Cluster
clustersAdd.desc: Click to create a new Kafka cluster connection. Enter a name and broker addresses to get started.

clustersCard.title: Cluster Cards
clustersCard.desc: Each card shows the cluster connection status. Right-click or use the action buttons to refresh, edit, delete, or disconnect.

topicsCreate.title: Create Topic
topicsCreate.desc: Click to create a new Kafka topic with partition count, replication factor, and other parameters.

topicsList.title: Topic List
topicsList.desc: Lists all topics in the current cluster with search and sort support.

topicsActions.title: Topic Actions
topicsActions.desc: View messages, view details, send messages, or delete topics.

messagesSearch.title: Message Search
messagesSearch.desc: Search for keywords within message content.

messagesPartition.title: Partition Selector
messagesPartition.desc: Select a specific partition to query, or query all partitions.

messagesMode.title: Query Mode
messagesMode.desc: Choose whether to start from the newest or oldest messages.

messagesTimeFilter.title: Advanced Filter
messagesTimeFilter.desc: Filter messages by time range. Supports manual time input or quick time range presets.

messagesTimePresets.title: Quick Time Range
messagesTimePresets.desc: Quickly select recent time ranges: 5 minutes, 15 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, or 1 day.

messagesCount.title: Message Count
messagesCount.desc: Set the maximum number of messages to return per partition per query.

messagesQuery.title: Execute Query
messagesQuery.desc: Fetch messages from Kafka. After per-partition data returns, filter results by query conditions and display.

messagesSend.title: Send Message
messagesSend.desc: Open the message send dialog to publish new messages to the current topic.

messagesExport.title: Export Messages
messagesExport.desc: Export queried messages as a JSON file saved locally.

messagesMore.title: More Actions
messagesMore.desc: Access more operations like sent history, consumer groups, and topic deletion.

messagesTableHeader.title: Message Table Header
messagesTableHeader.desc: Column headers for partition, offset, timestamp, key, and value. Click the timestamp to sort, drag column dividers to resize.

messagesTableRow.title: Message Row
messagesTableRow.desc: Preview of each message. Click to view details. Use arrow keys to navigate between rows.

messagesDetailPanel.title: Message Detail
messagesDetailPanel.desc: Full message content with Ctrl+F search, Ctrl+A select-all, JSON highlighting. Drag the top handle to resize the panel.

messagesValueViewFormat.title: View Format
messagesValueViewFormat.desc: Switch value display format: JSON (auto-formatted), Raw (plain text), or Hex (hexadecimal).

consumerGroupsList.title: Consumer Groups List
consumerGroupsList.desc: Shows all consumer groups with their state, lag, and other details.

consumerGroupsRefresh.title: Refresh
consumerGroupsRefresh.desc: Sync the latest consumer group status from the Kafka cluster.

consumerGroupsDetail.title: Consumer Group Details
consumerGroupsDetail.desc: Click a consumer group name to view details, including partition assignments and offsets.

favoritesList.title: Favorites List
favoritesList.desc: Shows your saved topics for quick access. Click to jump to the messages page.

favoritesAdd.title: Add to Favorites
favoritesAdd.desc: Add topics to favorites from the topic list or messages page for quick access later.

settingsNav.title: Settings Categories
settingsNav.desc: Browse and modify global settings by category.

settingsSections.title: Settings Items
settingsSections.desc: Manage language, theme, sidebar mode, system tray, auto-launch, and more.

schemaRegistryConfig.title: Configuration Area
schemaRegistryConfig.desc: Configure the Schema Registry connection URL and authentication credentials.

schemaRegistryActions.title: Action Buttons
schemaRegistryActions.desc: Test connection, save, or delete your Schema Registry configuration.

schemaRegistryTitle.title: Page Title
schemaRegistryTitle.desc: Schema Registry management entry point, configure and manage Schema Registry.

schemaRegistryNoCluster.title: No Cluster Selected
schemaRegistryNoCluster.desc: Select a connected Kafka cluster first to configure Schema Registry.

schemaRegistryNotConfig.title: Not Configured
schemaRegistryNotConfig.desc: Schema Registry is not yet configured for this cluster. Click the button to add configuration.

schemaRegistryConfigCard.title: Configuration Info Card
schemaRegistryConfigCard.desc: Shows current Schema Registry connection info including URL, username, and connection status.

schemaRegistryConfigActions.title: Config Actions
schemaRegistryConfigActions.desc: Edit or delete the Schema Registry connection configuration.

schemaRegistrySubjectsCard.title: Subjects List
schemaRegistrySubjectsCard.desc: Manage all registered Schema Subjects, with support for viewing, registering, and deleting.

schemaRegistrySubjectsTable.title: Subject Table
schemaRegistrySubjectsTable.desc: Displays each Subject version, type, compatibility, and other information.

schemaRegistryClusterDialog.title: Cluster Selector Dialog
schemaRegistryClusterDialog.desc: Select which Kafka cluster to manage Schema Registry for.

schemaRegistryConfigDialog.title: Configuration Dialog
schemaRegistryConfigDialog.desc: Set the Schema Registry connection URL and authentication info.

schemaRegistrySchemaDialog.title: Schema Details Dialog
schemaRegistrySchemaDialog.desc: View the full Schema content and metadata.

schemaRegistryRegisterDialog.title: Register Schema Dialog
schemaRegistryRegisterDialog.desc: Register a new Schema to the registry, supporting AVRO, Protobuf, and JSON formats.

sidebarViewSwitcher.title: View Switcher
sidebarViewSwitcher.desc: Toggle between Topics and Consumer Groups in the left sidebar navigation.

sidebarClustersBtn.title: Cluster Management
sidebarClustersBtn.desc: Navigate to the cluster management page to add, edit, or remove cluster connections.

sidebarFavoritesBtn.title: Favorites
sidebarFavoritesBtn.desc: Open the favorites page to manage your saved topics with group support.

sidebarSchemaBtn.title: Schema Registry
sidebarSchemaBtn.desc: Open the Schema Registry page to configure and manage schemas.

sidebarHistoryBtn.title: Browsing History
sidebarHistoryBtn.desc: View recently browsed topics and consumer groups.

sidebarClusterSelector.title: Cluster Filter
sidebarClusterSelector.desc: Click to open the cluster selection panel. Select or deselect clusters to filter the displayed data. When no clusters are selected, all clusters are shown.

sidebarRefresh.title: Refresh Data
sidebarRefresh.desc: Synchronize the latest topics or consumer groups from the Kafka cluster.

sidebarSearch.title: Search
sidebarSearch.desc: Search for topics or consumer groups by name within the current clusters. Results filter automatically as you type.

sidebarTopicList.title: Topic List
sidebarTopicList.desc: Displays all topics with their cluster. Click any topic to navigate to the messages page. Scrolling to the bottom automatically loads more items.

sidebarTopicName.title: Topic Name
sidebarTopicName.desc: Click the topic name to jump to the messages page and view the latest message details.

sidebarConsumerGroupList.title: Consumer Group List
sidebarConsumerGroupList.desc: Displays all consumer groups with their cluster. Click any consumer group to navigate to the details page showing partition assignments and offsets.

sidebarConsumerGroupName.title: Consumer Group Name
sidebarConsumerGroupName.desc: Click the consumer group name to jump to the details page showing partition assignments and offset information.

sidebarHealthDot.title: Health Indicator
sidebarHealthDot.desc: The dot on the left of each item shows the cluster connection status: green = connected, red = disconnected, yellow = unknown.

sidebarClusterBadge.title: Cluster Badge
sidebarClusterBadge.desc: The badge on the right shows which cluster each item belongs to, helping you distinguish topics or consumer groups with the same name across multiple clusters.

treeCollapseBtn.title: Collapse All
treeCollapseBtn.desc: Click to collapse all expanded clusters and folders.

treeClustersBtn.title: Cluster Management
treeClustersBtn.desc: Navigate to the cluster management page to add, edit, or remove cluster connections.

treeFavoritesBtn.title: Favorites
treeFavoritesBtn.desc: Open the favorites page to manage your saved topics.

treeSchemaBtn.title: Schema Registry
treeSchemaBtn.desc: Open the Schema Registry page to configure and manage schemas.

treeGroupSelector.title: Group Filter
treeGroupSelector.desc: Quickly filter clusters by group membership.

treeClusterNode.title: Cluster Node
treeClusterNode.desc: The dot on the left shows the connection status. Double-click the cluster node to expand and view its Topics and Consumer Groups.

treeClusterName.title: Cluster Name
treeClusterName.desc: The name of the Kafka cluster, displayed in the node header.

treeClusterHealthDot.title: Cluster Health Indicator
treeClusterHealthDot.desc: The dot on the left of the cluster name shows the connection status: green = connected, red = disconnected, yellow pulsing = refreshing.

treeTopicsFolder.title: Topics Folder
treeTopicsFolder.desc: Click to expand and view all topics under this cluster. The refresh button syncs the latest data from Kafka.

treeTopicSearch.title: Topic Search
treeTopicSearch.desc: Search for topics by name within the current cluster. Results filter automatically as you type.

treeTopicName.title: Topic Name
treeTopicName.desc: Click a topic name to jump to the messages page and view the latest messages.

treeTopicsRefresh.title: Refresh Topics
treeTopicsRefresh.desc: Synchronize the latest topic list from the Kafka cluster.

treeConsumerGroupsFolder.title: Consumer Groups Folder
treeConsumerGroupsFolder.desc: Click to expand and view all consumer groups under this cluster. The refresh button syncs the latest data from Kafka.

treeConsumerGroupSearch.title: Consumer Group Search
treeConsumerGroupSearch.desc: Search for consumer groups by name within the current cluster. Results filter automatically as you type.

treeConsumerGroupName.title: Consumer Group Name
treeConsumerGroupName.desc: Click a consumer group name to jump to the details page showing partition assignments and offsets.

treeConsumerGroupsRefresh.title: Refresh Consumer Groups
treeConsumerGroupsRefresh.desc: Synchronize the latest consumer group list from the Kafka cluster.

tcgTitle.title: Topic Consumer Groups
tcgTitle.desc: This page shows the offset information for all consumer groups consuming the specified topic.

tcgDataNotice.title: Data Source Notice
tcgDataNotice.desc: Consumer groups are from database history, offset data is fetched from Kafka in real-time.

tcgRefresh.title: Refresh Data
tcgRefresh.desc: Fetch the latest consumer group offset information.

tcgTable.title: Offsets Table
tcgTable.desc: Displays start offset, end offset, committed offset, and lag for each consumer group across partitions.

cgGroupName.title: Consumer Group Name
cgGroupName.desc: The name of the consumer group you are currently viewing.

cgState.title: State Badge
cgState.desc: Shows the current consumer group state: Stable, PreparingRebalance, Empty, etc.

cgRefresh.title: Refresh
cgRefresh.desc: Synchronize the latest consumer group offset data from the Kafka cluster.

cgActions.title: More Actions
cgActions.desc: Advanced operations like resetting offsets or deleting the consumer group.

cgList.title: Offsets List
cgList.desc: Shows offset information for this consumer group across all topics and partitions.

cgTableTopic.title: Topic Column
cgTableTopic.desc: Displays the topic name that this partition belongs to.

cgTablePartition.title: Partition Column
cgTablePartition.desc: Kafka partition number. Drag column dividers to resize.

cgTableLag.title: Lag Column
cgTableLag.desc: Consumer lag (end_offset - committed_offset). Green = no lag, red = significant lag.

cgTableStartOffset.title: Start Offset
cgTableStartOffset.desc: The earliest offset in the partition, representing the minimum consumable offset.

cgTableEndOffset.title: End Offset
cgTableEndOffset.desc: The latest offset in the partition, representing the maximum written offset.

cgTableCommittedOffset.title: Committed Offset
cgTableCommittedOffset.desc: The offset committed by the consumer group, indicating where consumption will resume next.

cgTableLastCommit.title: Last Commit Time
cgTableLastCommit.desc: The timestamp of the last offset commit by the consumer group, helping determine consumer activity.

cgTableRow.title: Data Row
cgTableRow.desc: Complete offset information for each partition including start, end, committed offsets, and last commit time.

cgEmptyState.title: No Data
cgEmptyState.desc: This consumer group has no offset records.

cgResetDialog.title: Reset Offsets
cgResetDialog.desc: Open the reset offset dialog from the actions menu. You can select a topic, partition, and reset target (earliest/latest/specific offset/timestamp).

cgNoGroup.title: No Group Selected
cgNoGroup.desc: Select a consumer group from the left sidebar to view details.

clustersTitle.title: Cluster Management
clustersTitle.desc: Manage all Kafka cluster connections here: add, edit, test, or disconnect.

clustersManageGroups.title: Manage Groups
clustersManageGroups.desc: Create and edit cluster groups to organize clusters by project or environment.

clustersStatus.title: Connection Status
clustersStatus.desc: Green = connected, red = connection failed, gray = disconnected.

clustersEditBtn.title: Edit Cluster
clustersEditBtn.desc: Modify cluster name, broker addresses, or timeout settings.

clustersDeleteBtn.title: Delete Cluster
clustersDeleteBtn.desc: Remove the cluster connection configuration. This does not affect the Kafka cluster itself.

clustersTestBtn.title: Test Connection
clustersTestBtn.desc: Verify the current cluster configuration can connect successfully.

clustersReconnectBtn.title: Reconnect
clustersReconnectBtn.desc: Immediately attempt to re-establish the connection to the Kafka cluster.

clustersTopicsBtn.title: View Topics
clustersTopicsBtn.desc: Jump to the Topics page showing all topics under this cluster.

clustersRefreshTopicsBtn.title: Refresh Topics
clustersRefreshTopicsBtn.desc: Trigger a background refresh of topic metadata for this cluster.

clustersAddTopicBtn.title: Add Topic
clustersAddTopicBtn.desc: Create a new Topic on this cluster.

clustersEmpty.title: No Clusters
clustersEmpty.desc: You haven't added any clusters yet. Click 'Add Cluster' to get started.

clustersError.title: Connection Error
clustersError.desc: There is an issue with the cluster connection. Please check the configuration and try again.

clustersModal.title: Add/Edit Cluster
clustersModal.desc: Fill in the cluster name and broker addresses, then click save.

clustersModalName.title: Cluster Name
clustersModalName.desc: A short name to identify this cluster in the UI.

clustersModalBrokers.title: Broker Addresses
clustersModalBrokers.desc: Kafka broker connection addresses, comma-separated (e.g., localhost:9092,localhost:9093).

clustersModalTest.title: Test Connection
clustersModalTest.desc: Verify broker addresses and settings before saving.

clustersModalSubmit.title: Save Cluster
clustersModalSubmit.desc: Save the cluster configuration and add it to the cluster list.

settingsTitle.title: Settings Page
settingsTitle.desc: Manage global settings like language, theme, sidebar mode, and more.

settingsLanguage.title: Language
settingsLanguage.desc: Switch between Chinese and English interface.

settingsTheme.title: Theme
settingsTheme.desc: Toggle between light and dark themes.

settingsSidebarMode.title: Sidebar Mode
settingsSidebarMode.desc: Switch between list and tree sidebar layouts. Also includes system tray and auto-launch settings.

settingsVersion.title: Version Info
settingsVersion.desc: View current version, author info, check for updates, or view app logs.

settingsVersionBadge.title: Version Number
settingsVersionBadge.desc: Click 5 times quickly to unlock developer features (view logs).

settingsImportExport.title: Import/Export
settingsImportExport.desc: Export all configuration as a JSON backup file, or import from a file to restore data.

settingsJsonHighlight.title: JSON Highlight Settings
settingsJsonHighlight.desc: Configure how JSON values are highlighted in messages.

favTitle.title: Favorites Page
favTitle.desc: Manage your saved topics with group support.

favGroupCard.title: Group Card
favGroupCard.desc: Click to expand or collapse a group to view saved topics.

favEditGroup.title: Edit Group
favEditGroup.desc: Modify the group name, description, and sort order.

favDeleteGroup.title: Delete Group
favDeleteGroup.desc: Remove the group and all favorites inside it.

favGroupSearch.title: Group Search
favGroupSearch.desc: Search for topics or notes within the current group.

favItemRow.title: Favorite Item
favItemRow.desc: Double-click to jump to the topic messages page.

favEditItem.title: Edit Favorite
favEditItem.desc: Change the favorite topic group, description, or sort order.

favDeleteItem.title: Delete Favorite
favDeleteItem.desc: Remove this topic from your favorites.

favEmptyState.title: No Favorites
favEmptyState.desc: You haven't created any favorite groups yet. Click 'Create Group' to get started.

favGroupModal.title: Group Edit Dialog
favGroupModal.desc: Set the group name, description, and sort priority.

favFavoriteModal.title: Favorite Edit Dialog
favFavoriteModal.desc: Move the favorite to another group or add a description.
